identity.author_byline: By Alex Montekristobelgrade, REIG Solar Technical Team
identity.product: RenergyWare — field-proven NEMA 4 / UL-listed SCADA + DAS enclosures

seo_limits.ENFORCEMENT: HARD BLOCKER — count characters exactly before marking any post done. Title outside 45–55 = blocker. Meta ≥ 155 = blocker. No exceptions.

voice.type: Technical authority — SCADA/DAS specialist writing for utility-scale solar professionals
voice.feel: Experienced SCADA engineer explaining field-proven process to a smart project manager who needs to make decisions. Technical, precise, evidence-driven. Never marketing.
rules: Open with a specific failure mode or problem — never a definition
Apply Measurement, Meaning, Control framework to every commissioning post
Cite at least 2–3 external standards (IEC, NIST, IEEE, NERC) per post
Every technical claim backed by a named standard or REIG field experience
State outcomes as facts: 'This ensures data is defensible' — not 'This will help you'
Use named specifics: 'NERC CIP-007 requires patches within 35 days' — not 'regulations require'
Include a dedicated Failure Modes section in every post
Tables for every comparison, checklist, and deliverable list — readers scan
Declare target audience in every post: 'for project managers, commissioning leads, SCADA engineers, and O&M teams'
Position RenergyWare as 'field-proven' — exact descriptor, never omit

required_elements: SEO Title: 50–60 chars exactly — count characters before finalizing
Meta description: < 155 chars (target 145–154) — count characters before finalizing
Focus keyword in: SEO title, meta description, H1, first paragraph, ≥30% of H2+H3 headings, ≥1 image alt
Keyword density: 0.65%–0.9% — calculate exact occurrences ÷ word count before marking done
Author byline immediately after H1 (visible in HTML body)
Key Takeaways box (5 bullets — 2+ must cite a standard or statistic with source inline)
Measurement, Meaning, Control framework referenced in every post
Failure Modes section — dedicated section, never omit
At least 1–2 comparison tables with <thead> markup
SVG charts: ALL text labels verified to fit their container before saving — no overflow allowed
FAQ: 5–6 Q&A pairs, 40–60 words each, fully self-contained answers
Where REIG fits section — 1–2 paragraphs, always mentions 'commissioning-ready'
Further reading: 4 internal links — none duplicating body links, all from SITEMAP.md
References section: at least 2–3 external standards with full citations
3–5 internal links verified against SITEMAP.md — no same-batch linking

technical_reference.name: RenergyWare
technical_reference.descriptor: field-proven — always use this exact word

internal_link_rules.format: /blog/{slug} — relative path only, no full domain
internal_link_rules.source: SITEMAP.md is the ONLY valid source of slugs

external_link_rules.verification: Fetch EVERY external link via WebFetch before delivery — zero broken or wrong-page links
external_link_rules.ieee_format: Always use stable URL: standards.ieee.org/standard/XXXX-YYYY.html — NEVER use internal doc ID URLs like /ieee/1815/5925/
external_link_rules.nerc_format: nerc.com/pa/Stand/Pages/ — may return 403 from scrapers but URL is valid; keep as-is
external_link_rules.standards_check: When fetching IEEE/IEC standards, verify the page title matches the expected standard number and topic
external_link_rules.failure_action: If a standards link is broken or wrong: find correct URL, replace, re-verify before saving

svg_rules.label_placement: Bar labels ALWAYS outside bars unless bar width > (char_count × 7px). Default: outside right. Never assume text fits — calculate.
svg_rules.text_overflow_check: MANDATORY pre-save: scan every text element. If label extends beyond bar or viewBox edge, fix before saving.
svg_rules.chart_type_rotation: Never repeat chart type in same post: horizontal bar → grouped bar → lollipop → Gantt → line → radar

preferred_sources: NREL PV O&M Best Practices 3rd Ed. — monitoring and O&M (nrel.gov)
FOA OSP installation guidance — thefoa.org (fiber installation)
off_limits: Implying REIG is a generalist contractor
Specific cost/price claims without value grounding
ERCOT-specific rules presented as universal grid requirements
last_feedback: 2026-04-01 NON-NEGOTIABLE: SEO Title strictly 50–60 chars. Meta description strictly < 155 chars. SVG labels must never overflow. All external links verified via WebFetch. Quality parity with BreathMastery posts is required.

2026-03-28 Inferred from full blog analysis: Every post uses Measurement/Meaning/Control framework. FAQ always 5–6 pairs. 'RenergyWare' always 'field-proven'. CTAs always practical and non-salesy. Never name competitors. Posts target 'project managers, commissioning leads, SCADA engineers, and O&M teams'.
